title: Literacy and cognitive transformation
first_prompt: If we are going into the features of orality, shouldn't we then examine literacy in the same way? We assume people know what literacy is, but having it spelled out in the same way might be enlightenin
summary: The conversation centered on defining the cognitive architecture of literacy, seeking a symmetrical analysis to the study of orality. The discussion progressed from the benefits of deep reading and sustained attention to proposing a "hybrid form" that merges episodic long-form content with genuine call and response. This led to a critical examination of collaborative authorship, where the conversation concluded by identifying seminal theorists, specifically naming Jack Goody and his work *The Domestication of the Savage Mind*, as essential references for understanding literacy's features.
